report.summary: Analysis complete for a 5-room floor plan. 37 structural elements were identified, of which 35 are load-bearing (walls, columns, slab). Material recommendations were generated using weighted tradeoff scoring — load-bearing elements prioritise strength and durability, while partition walls prioritise cost efficiency. 17 structural concern(s) require attention before construction. Estimated material cost: ₹6,126,118–₹7,827,819.

element_reports.explanation: RCC is recommended for this load bearing wall with a tradeoff score of 5.500. As a load-bearing element, strength (60%) and durability (30%) dominate the scoring. With a span of 46.20m (exceeding the 5m threshold for standard masonry), this material's very high strength and very high durability best match the structural demand. Steel Frame scores 5.500 — a delta of 0.000 below RCC. Steel Frame would be preferred in budget-constrained scenarios where the span is below 3m and structural risk is lower.

ranked_materials.name: RCC
ranked_materials.notes: Reinforced cement concrete, ideal for structural elements

ranked_materials.name: Steel Frame
ranked_materials.notes: Best for spans greater than 5m, high tensile strength

ranked_materials.name: Precast Concrete Panel
ranked_materials.notes: Factory-made precision, fast installation, excellent for structural walls

element_reports.explanation: Fly Ash Brick is recommended for this partition wall with a tradeoff score of 4.700. As a partition element, cost (60%) dominates since structural performance is secondary. With a span of 5.40m (exceeding the 5m threshold for standard masonry), this material's medium-high strength and high durability best match the structural demand. AAC Blocks scores 4.600 — a delta of 0.100 below Fly Ash Brick. AAC Blocks would be preferred in budget-constrained scenarios where the span is below 3m and structural risk is lower.

element_reports.explanation: RCC is recommended for this load bearing wall with a tradeoff score of 5.500. As a load-bearing element, strength (60%) and durability (30%) dominate the scoring. With a span of 18.30m (exceeding the 5m threshold for standard masonry), this material's very high strength and very high durability best match the structural demand. Steel Frame scores 5.500 — a delta of 0.000 below RCC. Steel Frame would be preferred in budget-constrained scenarios where the span is below 3m and structural risk is lower.

element_reports.explanation: Fly Ash Brick is recommended for this partition wall with a tradeoff score of 4.700. As a partition element, cost (60%) dominates since structural performance is secondary. With a span of 8.50m (exceeding the 5m threshold for standard masonry), this material's medium-high strength and high durability best match the structural demand. AAC Blocks scores 4.600 — a delta of 0.100 below Fly Ash Brick. AAC Blocks would be preferred in budget-constrained scenarios where the span is below 3m and structural risk is lower.

ranked_materials.name: Fly Ash Brick
ranked_materials.notes: Eco-friendly, uniform size, lower water absorption than red brick

ranked_materials.name: AAC Blocks
ranked_materials.notes: Lightweight, good thermal insulation, easy to cut

ranked_materials.notes: Good for non-structural walls, reduces dead load

concern_reports: CRITICAL: A load-bearing wall spanning 46.2m exceeds the 5m structural threshold for standard masonry. Without a steel frame or RCC beam, deflection under load can cause cracking or collapse. Recommend: steel frame primary structure or RCC beam with intermediate column.
